label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's think through this step by step. So the question is whether following these instructions will bring me back to the starting point. Let me visualize the steps. First, I start at a point, let's say facing north. The first instruction is to turn right. If I was facing north, turning right would make me face east. Then take 9 steps east. Then take 10 steps in the same direction (east) so total 19 steps east so far. Next instruction is to turn left. If I was facing east and turn left, I'll face north again. Take 10 steps north. Now I'm 10 steps north and 19 steps east from the starting point. Then turn right. Facing east again. Then "turn around" which would make me face west. Take 4 steps west. So from that point, moving west 4 steps. So my east-west position is 19 - 4 = 15 steps east. North-south position is still 10 steps north. So final position is 15 steps east and 10 steps north. That's not back to the starting point. So the answer should be No. Let me double-check. Starting facing north. Turn right
